story.title: Dr. Raj Patel and the Whispers from Space
story.text:
I am Dr. Raj Patel, an engineer at NASA. I communicate with spacecraft far from Earth. We use invisible waves to send and receive messages. These waves are part of the electromagnetic spectrum. This spectrum includes everything from radio waves to gamma rays.

I specialize in radio waves. Radio waves have a low frequency and long wavelength. Low frequency means they carry less energy. This makes them safe. It also helps them travel far. Their great penetration depth lets them pass through gas and dust in space. Our atmosphere also lets radio waves pass through. This is lucky for us. The atmosphere blocks dangerous high-energy waves like X-rays.

Our eyes cannot see radio waves. Our detection sensitivity for them is zero. So, we build giant dish antennas to detect the signals. A signal with a bigger amplitude is stronger and easier to catch. We listen carefully to these whispers from space. These invisible signals help us explore our solar system.
think_abouts: Why can't Dr. Patel use X-rays to communicate with spacecraft from Earth?
What would happen if a spacecraft's signal had a very low amplitude?
story.go_deeper: How does the Doppler Shift help Dr. Patel know if a spacecraft is moving towards or away from Earth?

margin_defs.definition: A person who designs and builds things.
Tools used to send or receive waves.

check_prompts: What kind of waves does Dr. Patel use to communicate, and why are they good for this job?

vocabulary.definition: The full family of light waves, organized by their energy level. Most are invisible to us.
vocabulary.use_sentence: Visible light is only a small part of the electromagnetic spectrum.

vocabulary.definition: How many waves pass by in one second. Higher frequency means more energy.
vocabulary.use_sentence: A radio wave has a low frequency, while an X-ray has a high frequency.

vocabulary.definition: The distance from the top of one wave to the top of the next wave.
vocabulary.use_sentence: Radio waves can have a long wavelength, as big as a building.

vocabulary.definition: How well a wave can pass through an object without being stopped.
vocabulary.use_sentence: X-rays have a high penetration depth, so they can pass through your skin.

discussion_cards.prompt: Dr. Patel uses radio waves. What are some other types of waves on the electromagnetic spectrum, and how do we use them in our daily lives?
discussion_cards.frame: One type of wave is ___, and we use it for ___.

discussion_cards.prompt: Why is it important for scientists like Dr. Patel to study things that are invisible to our eyes?
discussion_cards.frame: It is important to study invisible things because ___.

discussion_cards.prompt: The story says Earth's atmosphere blocks some waves. How is this both helpful and unhelpful for humans?
discussion_cards.frame: The atmosphere blocking waves is helpful because it ___, but it is unhelpful because it ___.

reflection_frames.knew: Before reading, I already knew that ___
reflection_frames.learned: From the story, I learned that ___
